'''Ian Hardman''' (born {{birth date|1985|12|8|df=y}}) is an English professional [[rugby league]] coach who is head coach of [[Keighley Cougars]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Widnes Vikings]].

==Background==
Hardman was born in [[Whiston, Merseyside]], England on 8 December 1985.<ref name=featherstonerovers>{{Cite web |title=Squad |url=http://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/squad/7 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180114020430/http://www.featherstonerovers.co.uk/squad/7 |archive-date=2018-01-14 |access-date=2026-01-29 |website=Featherstone Rovers}}</ref>

==Club career==
Ian Hardman was a try scorer in the England Under-17s win over the Australian Institute of sport side in December 2003. He made his début for St. Helens against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] on 15 August 2003 and made 46 appearances for St. Helens in total, scoring 15 tries, 11 goals and 8 drop goals. During the 2007 season, first-team chances at St. Helens were limited so Ian was loaned to [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], he was instrumental in keeping them in Super League in their first season, making 18 consecutive appearances. He returned to St. Helens, only to be signed by Widnes on a season long loan.<ref>{{cite news |title=Hardman agrees Vikings loan deal |url=https://www.runcornandwidnesworld.co.uk/sport/1887466.hardman-agrees-vikings-loan-deal/ |access-date=27 February 2026 |work=Runcorn and Widnes World |date=6 December 2007 |language=en}}</ref>

===Testimonial match===
Ian Hardman's [[Benefit (sports)|benefit]] [[Benefit season|season]]/[[testimonial match]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]], allocated by the [[Rugby Football League]], took place during the 2016 season.

==Coaching career==
Hardman moved into coaching after retiring from playing and was assistant coach at [[Featherstone Rovers]] between 2021 and 2025.<ref>{{cite news |title=Featherstone Rovers appoint new assistant coach – Total Rugby League |url=https://www.totalrl.com/featherstone-rovers-appoint-new-assistant-coach/ |access-date=3 February 2026 |work=Total RL |date=11 November 2021}}</ref> He moved to his first head coach role when he was appointed to that role at Keighley in February 2026.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Former St Helens and Hull KR star named as Keighley Cougars' new head coach |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ian-hardman-appointed-keighley-head-coach |access-date=27 February 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=4 February 2026 |language=en}}</ref>
